items.text: The Electric Supply Plan 20 Table of Contents is Consumers’ blueprint for compliance with Michigan’s 2023 Energy Law and for advancing sustainability objectives.
items.rationale: Descriptive statement about the Electric Supply Plan's purpose, framing/boilerplate language rather than a new risk.

items.text: To meet these objectives, Consumers is executing a multi-faceted strategy.
items.rationale: Generic transitional statement about strategy execution, no specific risk content.

items.text: This strategy involves taking steps to end the use of coal, including the retirement of the D.E.
items.rationale: Describes strategic steps (coal retirement) as background, not a newly identified risk itself.

items.text: Campbell, totaling 1,407 MW of nameplate capacity.
items.rationale: Factual capacity detail, not a risk statement.

items.text: Campbell is subject to temporary extensions under emergency orders issued by the U.S.
items.rationale: Introduces the fact that a planned coal plant retirement has been delayed by federal emergency orders, a specific operational/regulatory risk not previously described.

items.text: For a more detailed discussion of the emergency orders, see Item 7.
items.rationale: Cross-reference to another section, no substantive content.

items.text: Campbell Emergency Orders and Item 8.
items.rationale: Cross-reference fragment, boilerplate.

items.text: However, the retirement of J.H.
items.rationale: Sentence fragment continuing narrative, no new risk content on its own.

items.text: Campbell is subject to temporary extensions under emergency orders issued by the U.S.
items.rationale: Repeats the same emergency order/retirement delay fact already introduced elsewhere.

items.text: Under those emergency orders, Consumers has continued to operate these units for the benefit of MISO’s North and Central regions.
items.rationale: Adds detail on how the emergency orders are being used (supplying MISO regions), expanding the previously noted regulatory risk.

items.text: Of the 7,320 GWh generated by these units during 2025, Consumers supplied 3,608 GWh of electricity to MISO in order to comply with the emergency orders.
items.rationale: Provides quantitative detail (GWh supplied) further expanding the emergency order risk narrative.

items.text: For a more detailed discussion of the emergency orders, see Item 7.
items.rationale: Cross-reference, no new content.

items.text: Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations—Outlook—Consumers Electric Utility Outlook and Uncertainties—J.H.
items.rationale: Cross-reference to MD&A section, boilerplate.

items.text: Campbell Emergency Orders and Item 8.
items.rationale: Cross-reference fragment.

items.text: 6 In 2025, Consumers entered an agreement to sell the 13 hydroelectric dams that comprise the 35 generating units.
items.rationale: Discloses a new agreement to sell hydroelectric dam assets, a material corporate transaction not previously mentioned.

items.text: For a more detailed discussion of this transaction, see Item 8.
items.rationale: Cross-reference to Item 8, boilerplate.

items.text: 9 Reflects net delivered energy from storage operations, after accounting for charging losses.
items.rationale: Footnote clarifying methodology, not a risk.

items.text: 3 Reflects net delivered energy from storage operations, after accounting for charging losses.
items.rationale: Duplicate footnote clarification, not a risk.

items.text: Consumers planned to exit coal generation in 2025 but the retirement of J.H.
items.rationale: Reveals that the planned 2025 coal exit was delayed, a new operational/strategic risk regarding failure to meet stated timeline.

items.text: Campbell is subject to temporary extensions under emergency orders issued by the U.S.
items.rationale: Repeats the same emergency order extension fact already disclosed.

items.text: Of the 7,320 GWh generated by these units during 2025, Consumers supplied 3,608 GWh of electricity to MISO in order to comply with the emergency orders.
items.rationale: Duplicate of the GWh supply figure already given elsewhere.

items.text: For a more detailed discussion of the emergency orders, see Item 7.
items.rationale: Cross-reference, boilerplate.

items.text: Management’s Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations—Outlook—Consumers Electric Utility Outlook and Uncertainties—J.H.
items.rationale: Cross-reference to MD&A, boilerplate.

items.text: Campbell Emergency Orders and Item 8.
items.rationale: Cross-reference fragment.

items.text: Following the emergency orders, Consumers was able to utilize relationships with existing suppliers in order to procure additional supply and maintain railcar leases and transportation contracts past the planned shutdown date of May 2025.
items.rationale: Adds operational detail about how Consumers managed supply and contracts after the delayed shutdown, expanding the emergency order risk description.

items.text: Consumers has also set a goal to reduce customer greenhouse gas emissions by 25 percent by 2035.
items.rationale: States a sustainability goal, which is a business objective rather than a disclosed risk.

items.text: Consumers’ Natural Gas Delivery Plan, a rolling ten ‑ year investment plan to deliver safe, reliable, clean, and affordable natural gas to customers, outlines ways in which Consumers can make early progress toward these goals in a cost-effective manner, including energy waste reduction, carbon offsets, and renewable natural gas supply.
items.rationale: Describes the Natural Gas Delivery Plan and its goals, descriptive strategy language.

items.text: Consumers has already initiated work in these key areas by continuing to expand its energy waste reduction targets and by offering gas customers the ability to offset their carbon footprint associated with natural gas use by purchasing renewable natural gas and/or carbon credits associated with Michigan forest preservation.
items.rationale: Describes ongoing initiatives, not a new risk.

items.text: Consumers has renewable natural gas facilities under construction scheduled for commercial operation in 2026 and is monitoring regulatory developments and market conditions closely as part of its ongoing evaluation of the projects.
items.rationale: Describes construction progress and monitoring of regulatory developments, general descriptive language.

items.text: 2 This project began operations in December 2025.
items.rationale: Factual operational start date, not a risk statement.

items.text: 3 Represents a behind-the-meter system located on customer premises.
items.rationale: Footnote description of a system type, not a risk.

items.text: For additional details see Item 8.
items.rationale: Cross-reference to financial statements, boilerplate.

items.text: Financial Statements and Supplementary Data—Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements—Note 19, Variable Interest Entities.
items.rationale: Cross-reference to Note 19, boilerplate.

items.text: Rate proceedings and other regulatory actions may affect operations and financial results.
items.rationale: Generic risk factor heading about rate proceedings, standard boilerplate framing.

items.text: Financial Statements and Supplementary Data—Notes to the Consolidated Financial Statements—Note 3, Regulatory Matters.
items.rationale: Cross-reference to Note 3, boilerplate.

items.text: Secretary of Energy has the authority to issue emergency orders for power plants under section 202(c) of the Federal Power Act.
items.rationale: Explains the specific legal authority (Federal Power Act section 202(c)) enabling government intervention in plant operations, a new regulatory risk mechanism disclosed.

items.text: This provision allows the U.S.
items.rationale: Continuation fragment of the regulatory authority description.

items.text: Secretary of Energy to temporarily alter the operation of the electricity system during emergencies.
items.rationale: Further elaborates on the scope of the Secretary of Energy's emergency authority, expanding the newly disclosed regulatory risk.

items.text: Consumers’ Electric Supply Plan, its long-term strategy for delivering safe, reliable, affordable, clean, and equitable energy to its customers, is outlined in its integrated resource plan and incorporates Consumers’ Renewable Energy Plan.
items.rationale: Descriptive statement about the Electric Supply Plan's components, background information.

items.text: The Electric Supply Plan is Consumers’ blueprint for compliance with Michigan’s 2023 Energy Law and for advancing sustainability objectives.
items.rationale: Duplicate of the earlier descriptive statement about the Electric Supply Plan's purpose.
